document: MAS Notice 648 Issuance of Covered Bonds by Banks Incorporated in Singapore 20240516 (Singapore - Row 13)

sections.heading: Only a bank incorporated in Singapore may issue covered bonds in Singapore. A bank
sections.title: Singapore banks only issue covered bonds within Singapore
sections.summary: Only Singapore-incorporated banks may issue covered bonds in Singapore. Banks outside Singapore cannot issue covered bonds through Singapore branches. This restricts the issuer to Singapore entities for covered bond issuance within the country.
sections.dtia_summary: Restricts covered bond issuance to Singapore-incorporated banks, preventing cross-border issuance via Singapore branches. Relevant for DTIA as it defines permissible issuers.

requirements.text: Only Singapore-incorporated banks may issue covered bonds in Singapore
Banks outside Singapore must not issue covered bonds through Singapore branches

sections.source_quote: Only a bank incorporated in Singapore may issue covered bonds in Singapore. A bank incorporated outside Singapore must not issue any covered bonds through its branch in Singapore.

sections.heading: MAS Notice 628 on Securitisation shall not apply to a bank incorporated in Singapore
sections.title: MAS Notice 628 does not apply to Singapore banks' covered bonds
sections.summary: MAS Notice 628 on securitisation does not apply to Singapore-incorporated banks when issuing covered bonds under this Notice. However, all other securitisation activities remain subject to MAS Notice 628.
sections.dtia_summary: Exempts Singapore banks from MAS Notice 628 for covered bond issuance, but retains securitisation obligations for other activities. Relevant for DTIA as it clarifies scope of exemption.

requirements.text: MAS Notice 628 on securitisation shall not apply to Singapore-incorporated banks in relation to covered bond issuance
All other securitisation activities remain subject to MAS Notice 628

sections.source_quote: MAS Notice 628 on Securitisation shall not apply to a bank incorporated in Singapore in relation to its issuance of covered bonds in accordance with the requirements in this Notice. For the avoidance of doubt, all requirements of MAS Notice 628 shall continue to apply to such a bank incorporated in Singapore in respect of its other securitisation activities.

sections.heading: In this Notice—
sections.title: Definitions for covered bonds and related terms
sections.summary: Defines key terms including 'covered bonds', 'cover pool', 'MAS Bills', 'Memorandum of Compliance', 'Singapore Government Securities', and 'SPV' for the purpose of covered bond issuance. These definitions establish the scope and structure of covered bonds.
sections.dtia_summary: Provides precise definitions critical for understanding covered bond structures and compliance requirements. Essential for DTIA as it clarifies terms used in subsequent sections.

requirements.text: Covered bonds mean bonds, notes or debentures issued by a bank or SPV with payment secured by a cover pool
Cover pool means assets legally owned by the bank or SPV for securing covered bond liabilities
MAS Bills means securities issued by MAS under the Monetary Authority of Singapore Act 1970
Memorandum of Compliance means a bank's document showing compliance with requirements
Singapore Government Securities means debt securities issued by Singapore Government
SPV means special purpose vehicles incorporated in Singapore for covered bonds

sections.source_quote: In this Notice— "covered bonds" means any bonds, notes or other debentures issued by a bank or an SPV where payment of the liabilities to the holders of such covered bonds and any liabilities arising from the enforcement of the rights of the holders of the covered bonds are: (a) secured by a cover pool; and (b) recoverable from the bank regardless of whether the cover pool is sufficient to pay off such liabilities; "cover pool" in relation to an issuance of covered bonds, means a pool of assets that are – (a) legally or beneficially owned or legally and beneficially owned by the bank or an SPV, (b) held by the bank as trustee, or a replacement trustee, on behalf of an SPV, or (c) both, for the purposes of securing the payment of: (i) the liabilities to the holders of the covered bonds; (ii) any liabilities arising from the enforcement of the rights of the holders of the covered bonds; and (iii) any liabilities to third party service providers appointed for the operation and administration of the covered bond programme; "MAS Bills" means any securities issued by the Monetary Authority of Singapore under the Monetary Authority of Singapore Act 1970; "Memorandum of Compliance" means a memorandum prepared by the bank setting out how it has complied with each of the requirements in paragraphs 6(a) to (i), and 8(a) of this Notice; "Singapore Government Securities" means any debt securities issued by the Singapore Government under any written law; and "SPV" means any special purpose vehicle incorporated or established in Singapore for the primary purpose of issuing covered bonds or holding the cover pool in relation to such covered bonds or both.

sections.heading: The expressions used in this Notice shall, except where expressly defined in this Notice
sections.title: Cover pool assets and encumbrance limits for covered bonds
sections.summary: Singapore-incorporated banks must ensure cover pools for covered bonds consist of residential mortgage loans and specific assets. The cover pool must not exceed 10% of the bank's total assets, with strict limits on cash, MAS Bills, and Singapore Government Securities. Banks must maintain capital against cover pool exposures and conduct annual property valuations.
sections.dtia_summary: Sets strict limits on cover pool composition and size relative to bank assets, critical for DTIA as it defines permissible data structures and cross-border risks.

requirements.text: Cover pool must comprise residential mortgage loans secured by residential property
Cover pool may include loans secured by same residential property
Cover pool may include intangible properties forming part of security for residential mortgage loans
Cover pool may include interest held by bank as trustee for residential mortgage loans
Cover pool may include derivatives for hedging risks from covered bond issuance
Cover pool may include cash (including foreign currency)
Cover pool may include Singapore Government Securities
Cover pool may include MAS Bills
Aggregate value of cash, MAS Bills, and Singapore Government Securities must not exceed 15% of residential mortgage loans and cover pool assets
Aggregate value of residential mortgage loans and cover pool assets must not exceed 10% of bank's total assets
Bank must continue to hold capital against cover pool exposures per MAS Notice 637
Bank must conduct annual valuation of residential properties securing loans
Bank must seek legal advice for foreign property securing loans to ensure rights of covered bond holders
Cover pool value must be at least 103% of outstanding nominal amount of covered bonds

sections.source_quote: A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through an SPV, comply with or ensure that the SPV complies with, as the case may be, the following requirements at all times: (a) the cover pool must comprise mortgage loans secured by residential property... (b) the aggregate value of assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(v) to (vii) must not exceed 15% of the aggregate value of the residential mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii) included in the cover pool; (c) the aggregate value of – (i) the residential mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii) included in cover pools for all covered bonds issued by the bank, SPV, or both the bank and SPV, as the case may be; and (ii) the residential mortgage loans and the assets referred to in paragraph 6(a)(i) to (vii), not falling within sub-paragraph (i) transferred by the bank to the SPV, must not exceed 10% of the value of the total assets of the bank at all times.

sections.heading: For the purposes of determining the total assets of the bank under paragraph 6(c)(B) —
sections.title: Asset computation for regulatory requirements
sections.summary: Banks must compute assets used for regulatory requirements under the Banking Act 1970 without haircuts or adjustment factors. Banks may exclude assets with the lowest haircuts first for regulatory requirements, and must notify MAS before changing computation methods.
sections.dtia_summary: Defines precise asset computation methods critical for DTIA as it affects how assets are measured across borders for regulatory compliance.

requirements.text: Bank must compute assets for regulatory requirements under Banking Act 1970 without haircuts
Bank must compute assets for regulatory requirements under Banking Act 1970 without adjustment factors
Bank may exclude assets with lowest haircuts first for regulatory requirements
Bank may exclude assets with highest adjustment factors first for regulatory requirements
Bank must notify MAS at least three business days before changing computation methods

sections.source_quote: For the purposes of determining the total assets of the bank under paragraph 6(c)(B) — (a) the bank incorporated in Singapore must comply with all of the following: (i) the bank must compute the assets which the bank uses to meet the regulatory requirements under section 38 of the Banking Act 1970 without applying any haircuts specified in MAS Notice 649; (ii) the bank must compute the assets which the bank uses to meet the regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 1970 without applying any adjustment factors specified in any notice in writing issued under section 40(1) of the Banking Act 1970; (b) the bank incorporated in Singapore may do all of the following: (i) where the bank holds assets in excess of that which the bank is required to hold to meet the regulatory requirements mentioned in paragraph 6(c)(B), the bank need only exclude the minimum amount of assets the bank is required to hold to meet the regulatory requirements mentioned in paragraph 6(c)(B); (ii) where the bank holds more than one type of asset which can be used to meet the regulatory requirements under section 38 of the Banking Act 1970, for the purposes of computing the relevant assets, the bank may exclude assets with the lowest haircuts first, followed by assets with the next lowest haircut, and so on; (iii) where a bank holds more than one type of asset which can be used to meet the regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 1970, for the purposes of computing the assets which the bank uses to meet the regulatory requirements under section 40 of the Banking Act 1970, the bank may exclude assets with the highest adjustment factor first, followed by assets with the next highest adjustment factor, and so on.

sections.heading: A bank incorporated in Singapore which issues covered bonds must only use its own
sections.title: Cover pool assets must be from bank's own entities
sections.summary: Singapore-incorporated banks must use only their own assets to form cover pools, not assets from other entities in their banking group. Subsidiaries must also use their own assets for cover pools.
sections.dtia_summary: Ensures cover pools are formed from single entity assets, reducing cross-border data transfer risks in covered bond structures.

requirements.text: Bank must use only its own assets to form cover pools, not assets from other banking group entities
Subsidiaries must use only their own assets to form cover pools, not assets from other banking group entities

sections.source_quote: A bank incorporated in Singapore which issues covered bonds must only use its own assets, and not that of other entities in its banking group (as defined in MAS Notice 637) to form the cover pool and must ensure that every of its subsidiary which intends to issue covered bonds only uses the subsidiary's own assets, and not that of other entities in the banking group to form the cover pool.

sections.heading: A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through
sections.title: Governance and risk management for covered bond issuers
sections.summary: Singaporean banks must implement robust governance structures, including identifying approval authorities, conducting annual asset coverage tests, and performing regular stress tests to manage risks from covered bond issuance. The bank or SPV must ensure board and senior management are responsible for risk assessment and adherence to risk management processes. This applies to banks issuing covered bonds directly or through special purpose vehicles.
sections.dtia_summary: Requires specific risk management controls and oversight mechanisms for covered bond programs, impacting cross-border data transfers involving financial assets and collateral.

requirements.text: The bank or SPV must have appropriate governance arrangements including identifying approval authority
The bank or SPV must perform regular asset coverage tests annually to ensure collateral quality and over-collateralisation
The bank or SPV must conduct regular stress tests on risks including default, prepayment, currency, interest rate, counterparty and liquidity risks
The bank or SPV must ensure board and senior management are responsible for risk assessment and adherence to risk management processes
The bank or SPV must appoint an external auditor qualified under Companies Act 1967 as cover pool monitor
The cover pool monitor must verify annual compliance with paragraphs 6(a) to (i), 6A, 6B, 6C and 6D
The cover pool monitor must verify the bank or SPV maintains an accurate register of assets in the cover pool
The cover pool monitor must assess annual adequacy of risk management processes and internal controls for covered bonds
The cover pool monitor must submit certified annual report on compliance with paragraphs 8(b)(i) to (iii)
The cover pool monitor must report immediately to MAS if bank or SPV breaches any conditions
The bank must obtain legal opinion confirming assets in cover pool are beyond reach in insolvency
The bank must disclose consequences of asset transfers to borrowers regarding mortgage loans and deposits
The bank must disclose results of asset coverage tests and cover pool characteristics quarterly

sections.source_quote: A bank incorporated in Singapore must, when issuing covered bonds itself or through an SPV, comply or ensure that the SPV complies with, as the case may be, the following conditions to manage the risks arising from the issuance of covered bonds: (a) the bank or SPV, as the case may be, must have in place appropriate governance arrangements (such as identifying the approval authority within the bank or SPV, where the bank uses an SPV to issue covered bonds, with respect to the covered bond programme), performing regular and in any case, on an annual basis at the minimum, asset coverage tests (

sections.heading: This Notice shall take immediate effect.
sections.title: Effective date of the notice
sections.summary: This section states that the MAS Notice 648 takes immediate effect upon issuance. It does not impose any operational requirements but establishes the timeline for implementation of the covered bond issuance rules.
sections.source_quote: This Notice shall take immediate effect.
